2606.27472.title: Supersede: Diagnosing and Training the Memory-Update Gap in LLM Agents
2606.27472.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents operate over long, multi-session interactions in which facts change: a user moves, a price updates, a plan is revised. Acting correctly requires using the current value of a fact and discarding values that have been superseded. We isolate this ability on real conversational data and show that it is a distinct, unsolved failure. On the knowledge-update subset of LongMemEval, replacing an agent's full context with a bounded, self-maintained memory drops accuracy from 92% to 77% even on a frontier model (gpt-5.4), a gap that is statistically significant (paired McNemar p<0.005) and persists across model scale while full-context accuracy saturates near 92%. The bottleneck is therefore memory maintenance, not comprehension, and is not closed by a stronger model. We then ask whether this is merely an undersized memory, and find it is not: as the conversation grows 24x, accuracy falls further (from 68% to 28%), and granting the agent proportionally more memory yields no detectable recovery (28% to 28%, n=25). The failure scales with the length of the conversation, not the compression ratio. We release Supersede, an open reinforcement-learning environment (on the verifiers / prime-rl stack) that turns this measurement into a training signal: agents are rewarded for answering from the current value and penalized for stale ones. Finally, we close the loop and show the gap is trainable: GRPO fine-tuning a small open model (Qwen2.5-3B) on this environment nearly doubles its held-out supersession accuracy on real, unseen conversations (9.0% to 16.7%, a single run), along a monotonic checkpoint curve indicating the learned policy, not the harness, carries the gain. To our knowledge this is the first trainable environment whose reward targets temporal fact-currency, and the first evidence the supersession gap can be trained down, not only measured.
2606.27472.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理在長期的多會話互動中運作，其中事實會發生變化：用戶移動，價格更新，計劃被修訂。正確行動需要使用事實的當前值並丟棄已被取代的值。我們在真實的對話數據中孤立出這種能力，並顯示這是一個明顯且未解決的失敗。在 LongMemEval 的知識更新子集上，將代理的完整上下文替換為有限的自我維護記憶，即使在前沿模型（gpt-5.4）上，準確率也從 92% 降至 77%，這一差距在統計上是顯著的（配對 McNemar p<0.005），並且在模型規模上持續存在，而完整上下文的準確率接近 92% 時則飽和。因此，瓶頸在於記憶維護，而非理解，並且更強的模型無法解決這一問題。我們接著詢問這是否僅僅是記憶不足，結果發現並非如此：隨著對話增長 24 倍，準確率進一步下降（從 68% 降至 28%），而給予代理成比例更多的記憶並未帶來可檢測的恢復（28% 到 28%，n=25）。失敗隨著對話的長度而擴大，而非壓縮比。我們發布了 Supersede，一個開放的強化學習環境（在 verifiers / prime-rl 堆棧上），將這一測量轉化為訓練信號：代理因從當前值回答而獲得獎勵，因使用過時的值而受到懲罰。最後，我們關閉了循環，並顯示這一差距是可訓練的：在這一環境中對一個小型開放模型（Qwen2.5-3B）進行 GRPO 微調，幾乎將其在真實未見對話中的超越準確率翻倍（9.0% 到 16.7%，一次運行），沿著單調的檢查點曲線，表明學習的策略，而非裝置，帶來了增益。據我們所知，這是第一個獎勵目標為時間事實貨幣的可訓練環境，也是第一個證據表明超越差距可以被訓練縮小，而不僅僅是測量。
2606.27472.author: Vedant Patel et.al.
